body:
## Summary

- `8% of $50` returned `0.08` instead of `4 USD`. The keyword `of` was not recognised by the lexer, so the parser consumed `8%` (= 0.08) and silently dropped `$50`.
- Added `Of` to `TokenKind`, recognised it in `scan_identifier`, and extended `parse_unary()` so `N% of X` desugars to `(N/100) * X` â€” identical to the already-working `N% * X` form.
- All existing percentage expressions are unaffected.

## Reproduction

```
8% of $50   â†’  was: 0.08   now: 4 USD
10% of 200  â†’  was: error  now: 20
50% of 80   â†’  was: error  now: 40
```

## Root cause

`src/grammar/lexer.rs` had no `Of` token, so `"of"` fell through to `Identifier("of")`.  `parse_unary()` returned `8/100` and the trailing tokens were discarded.

## Fix (3 files changed)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/grammar/lexer.rs` | Add `Of` variant; map `"of"` to it in `scan_identifier` |
| `src/grammar/token_parser.rs` | After `%`, check for `Of` and multiply by the RHS |
| `tests/issue_145_percent_of_tests.rs` | 7 regression tests (core bug + equivalences + backward compat) |

## Test plan

- [x] `8% of $50` â†’ `4 USD`
- [x] `8% * $50` â†’ `4 USD` (unchanged)
- [x] `10% of 200` â†’ `20`
- [x] `50% of 80` â†’ `40`
- [x] `100% of 42` â†’ `42`
- [x] `50%` â†’ `0.5` (standalone percent unchanged)
- [x] `3% * 50` â†’ `1.5` (multiply form unchanged)
- [x] Full test suite: 0 failures

## Case study

`docs/case-studies/issue-145/README.md` â€” timeline, root cause analysis, requirements, and solution rationale.


Fixes #145
title: fix: support 'N% of X' syntax as (N/100) * X (issue #145)
